claim: World Bank Groundswell report projects up to 40 million internal climate migrants in South Asia by 2050 under pessimistic scenarios, primarily from agricultural areas affected by water stress and crop failure.

source.title: Groundswell: Acting on Internal Climate Migration

method: Reviewed Groundswell methodology, regional projections, and driver analysis.
limitations: Projections are modeled from biophysical and demographic scenarios, not observed migration flows.
Sub-national projections have wide uncertainty ranges.

claim: A 2021 review found moderate but significant correlations between environmental change (rainfall deficit, temperature increase, flood frequency) and out-migration at district scale in South Asia, with economic drivers explaining the majority of migration variance.

source.title: Environmental change and migration in South Asia: a systematic review

method: Reviewed systematic search, correlation estimates, and attribution methodology.
limitations: Census data is decadal and cannot capture short-term displacement.
Attribution to climate versus economic drivers is inherently uncertain.
